children.text: > Dr. Fiona Vernal begins the interview with Elizabeth 'Libbie' Merrow on May 11th, 2023, in Seabury, outdoors in front of a koi pond. Dr. Vernal prompts Libbie to share her childhood experiences.

> Libbie describes her privileged childhood, her father's profession as an obstetrician, and her family's involvement with the Congregational Church. Her mother migrated from Canada, and Libbie details their family’s lifestyle and her mother's health issues with tuberculosis.

> The discussion includes the names and backgrounds of Libbie's parents, Anne Etta Kearney Moses and Sanford. Libbie explains her mother's migration from Canada due to her brother's new job and family circumstances.

> Libbie recounts adventurous childhood memories including breaking into empty houses during the Great Depression, and playing imaginative games with friends. She shares how outdoor activities and reading were integral parts of her childhood.

> Libbie talks about her teenage years, her friendship with Mary, and the neighborhood dynamics. She recalls being surrounded by boys and having certain family hopes, like her father wanting her to become a doctor.

> Libbie discusses meeting her husband George, her decision to prioritize marriage over education, and their experiences in Seattle. She reflects on her academic performance and her path after marriage.
> The interview covers George’s family background, their common ancestral connections, and his career shifts from Boeing to other companies. The impact of their shared history is discussed.

> Libbie talks about their life on Scotland Road, the neighborhood dynamics, and how the area developed over the years. She describes the community, family activities, and their efforts in integrating the neighborhood.

> The conversation shifts to Bloomfield’s demographic changes over time, the effects of school integration, and how the racial makeup of the town evolved. Libbie reflects on the impact of white flight.

> Libbie shares her experiences and reflections on race, mentioning specific programs and her personal evolution in understanding racial issues. She highlights church involvement and personal insights.

> Libbie discusses the efforts to integrate their church, the challenges faced, and her personal growth through sacred conversations about race. She reflects on community activism and social involvement.
> Libbie talks about her journey to understanding racial dynamics and societal changes over the decades. She reflects on the role of education, historical context, and personal biases.

> The interview concludes with Libbie reflecting on Bloomfield's development, changing demographics, and thoughts on future challenges. She discusses the impact of urbanization and her views on community evolution.

story.title: Libbie Merrow Oral History.WAV
story.description: In this interview, Elizabeth "Libbie" Merrow discusses her life experiences, covering her privileged upbringing, family history, and reflections on living in Bloomfield, Connecticut, a town known for its diversity. Key themes include her family dynamics, the evolution of her neighborhood, race relations, and her participation in community and church activities. She talks about her challenges and discomforts with racial dynamics and societal changes over time. Issues such as school integration, white flight, and community activism are explored, along with broader reflections on societal progress and segregation. The conversation provides a nuanced perspective on personal and community history amidst ongoing social changes.
